what_it_does: A static, high-fidelity HTML/CSS mockup of a proposed desktop information-architecture shell called the commander's bridge. It renders a 1440px-wide three-zone layout (left palette, centre board, right inspector) plus a docked bottom RHM conversation pane, using sample nodes, wires, state colours, and data-ui-ref addresses to show how a selected subject (the ask node) would be visible simultaneously across board, inspector, and RHM.
observations: Fixed 1440px viewport, full-width desktop mockup with a dark warm-gold theme using CSS custom properties.
Grid layout: toolbar / rail / board / inspect / rhm.
Left rail is a collapsible palette of draggable chips grouped by process, content, and config slot.
Board contains SVG wires linking absolute-positioned node cards with state skins: ran, cached, await, failed, building, idle, live, empty.
Selected ask node gets a gold ring and lift to demonstrate simultaneity with inspector and RHM.
Board also shows a static context menu, hover-preview bubble, zoom controls, and a teach-mode spotlight.
Right inspector shows the selected node's config rows, rendered output block, and a first-class grow-the-interface affordance.
Address-help region explains the selected locus and uses progressively-disclosed disclosure rows for comments, versions, etc.
Queue/review region shows review/decision/question cards with actions.
Bottom RHM pane has a living orb, conversation log with co-presence indicator, text/voice input, and a tune strip with knobs, sparkline, and activity feed.
No JavaScript; interactions are implied by CSS and static annotations.
data-ui-ref attributes are sprinkled throughout to name UI loci (e.g. ui://canvas/node/ask).
standards_outs: The simultaneity concept: the same address ui://canvas/node/ask is surfaced on the board, in the inspector, and in the RHM pane.
Surface-everything rule: latent capabilities are exposed as recognisable collapsed affordances (chips, counts, sparklines, one-row ledgers) rather than deep junk drawers.
State-by-colour node vocabulary with a two-vivid + one-muted split (gold ran/live, amber await, coral fail, taupe cache, bronze building).
First-class 'grow the interface itself' affordance inside the inspector.
RHM orb as a living presence indicator with ripple animation.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for a tokenised warm-gold colour system.
Semantic HTML: aside for rails, main for board, section for regions.
data-ui-ref attributes encode addressed UI loci.
Inline documentation comments explain purpose, constraints, and design rationale.
concerns: File is truncated in the provided excerpt, so the full address-help and RHM content are incomplete.
Fixed 1440px width and absolute positioning make it a non-responsive, desktop-only artifact.
No JavaScript, so all interactivity (drag, collapse, teach spotlight, RHM input) is static or CSS-only.
Some data-ui-ref values appear cut off or malformed due to truncation (e.g. ui:/).
Heavy inline CSS and hand-coded pixel coordinates will be hard to maintain as a real implementation.
notes: A polished visual prototype meant for stakeholder reaction before engineering, encoding the proposed desktop IA, interaction zones, state vocabulary, and gold living-instrument aesthetic in one static HTML file.
questions: What is the full content after the truncation point?
Are the data-ui-ref values intended to be a real routing/addressing scheme, or just mockup annotations?
Is there a companion JavaScript file that brings the drag, collapse, teach, and RHM interactions to life?
How is the RHM pane actually bound to the selected node's address in the real system?
What does the underlying IA-PROPOSAL.md specify beyond the desktop section?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to let Tim react by sight to the proposed desktop IA before the real build; the file matches that by being a static, annotated visual mockup of the proposed three-zone layout and RHM pane.
apparent_themes: Simultaneity and legibility of a selected subject across zones.
Surface latent capabilities without creating deep junk drawers.
Status communicated by colour and light rather than text alone.
Living-instrument aesthetic (aurora, orb, gold glow, breathing animations).
Addressability of every UI locus via ui:// references.
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate a coherent desktop command interface where the current subject is always readable in multiple places at once, while keeping every real-but-latent capability visible as a small, recognisable affordance rather than hiding it in menus.
novelty: Explicit 'surface everything' design rule paired with collapsed-but-recognisable affordances; a three-zone simultaneity binding built around a single ui:// address; a docked RHM conversation pane with a presence orb and co-presence chip; and a first-class 'grow the interface' control surfaced inside the inspector.
contribution: Provides a concrete, annotated visual reference for the proposed desktop IA: zone layout, toolbar grouping, palette taxonomy, board node states and wiring, inspector structure, address-help pattern, queue cards, RHM conversation pane, and aesthetic tokens. It also seeds a UI-address map via data-ui-ref attributes that an implementation could adopt or debate.
summary_for_embedding: Static high-fidelity HTML/CSS mockup of a proposed desktop command-bridge interface with a three-zone layout, state-coded node board, inspector, address-help, queue, and docked RHM conversation pane.
intention_for_embedding: To let stakeholders preview and react to the proposed desktop information architecture before building it, especially the simultaneity of a selected node across board, inspector, and RHM.
